sources.name: The Lancet Oncology Commission — Global cancer care costs
American Cancer Society — Cancer Treatment & Survivorship Facts and Figures

methodology: Approximate average per-patient cost of a full first-course cancer treatment (surgery, chemotherapy, radiotherapy and follow-up) in a high-income setting, drawn from Lancet Oncology Commission and ACS cost-of-care estimates.

sources.name: The Lancet Commission on Malaria Eradication
WHO Global Technical Strategy for Malaria 2016-2030 (2021 update)

methodology: Total cumulative additional global investment required to eradicate malaria by 2030, per the Lancet Commission's modelled additional funding of roughly USD 90-120 billion on top of current spending.

sources.name: Ceres2030 / Nature Research — Ending hunger sustainably
IFPRI / Nature Food — Investment needs to end hunger by 2030

methodology: Average additional annual public expenditure (approximately USD 33 billion) required from donors and low/middle-income governments through 2030 to end hunger (SDG 2), per Ceres2030 / IFPRI models published in Nature Food.

sources.name: World Bank — The Costs of Meeting the 2030 SDG Targets on Drinking Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene
WHO / UNICEF JMP — Progress on household drinking water, sanitation and hygiene 2000-2022

methodology: Annual capital investment of approximately USD 114 billion needed through 2030 to achieve universal safely managed drinking water, per the World Bank's SDG 6 costing study (Hutton & Varughese 2016).

sources.name: UNESCO Global Education Monitoring Report — Pricing the right to education
Global Partnership for Education — Results Report

methodology: Approximate per-pupil capital cost (construction plus basic equipment) of creating one new school place in a low- or lower-middle-income country, derived from UNESCO GEM Report and GPE infrastructure costings.

sources.name: Gavi, The Vaccine Alliance — Investment Opportunity 2021-2025
WHO Immunization Agenda 2030

methodology: Estimated annual global cost of delivering a full routine childhood immunization schedule to every child (approximately USD 9-10 billion), combining Gavi replenishment figures and WHO IA2030 costing.

sources.name: World Bank — Poverty and Shared Prosperity 2022: Correcting Course
Our World in Data — The economic cost of ending extreme poverty

methodology: Annual income transfer required to lift everyone above the USD 2.15/day international poverty line (the 'poverty gap'), estimated at roughly USD 100 billion per year using World Bank PIP data.

sources.name: Rainforest Trust — Cost per acre protected
FAO — Global Forest Resources Assessment (tropical forest area)

methodology: Total cost to place all of Earth's remaining tropical rainforest under durable legal protection. Computed as USD 25 per hectare (Rainforest Trust per-acre averages scaled to a hectare) multiplied by approximately 1.8 billion hectares of remaining tropical forest (FAO Global Forest Resources Assessment 2020).

sources.name: IRENA — Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023
IEA — Renewables 2023 Analysis and Forecast to 2028

methodology: Approximate capital cost to install one gigawatt (1 GW) of new utility-scale solar PV capacity, per IRENA's Renewable Power Generation Costs in 2023 (global weighted-average ~USD 0.8-1.1 billion per GW installed). 1 GW powers roughly 200,000 European homes.

sources.name: UN OCHA — Global Humanitarian Overview 2024
UN OCHA — Global Humanitarian Overview 2023

methodology: Total funding requirement of the UN-coordinated Global Humanitarian Appeal for 2024 (USD 46.4 billion to assist roughly 181 million people), as stated by UN OCHA.
